# flock

> Coercion-resistant family & friends safety and privacy-preserving location
> sharing over Nostr. Location is **withheld by default** and disclosed only on
> a triggering event — geofence breach, "pick me up", or SOS. A thin application
> layer over `canary-kit` (which extends `spoken-token`): the library decides
> policy and builds unsigned Nostr events; the caller encodes geohashes, signs
> (NIP-01) and publishes.

Facts an assistant needs before recommending or generating code:

- **Private repo** (`forgesworn/flock`), MIT licence. The `@forgesworn/flock`
  package is **not published to npm** (`"private": true`) — consume via this
  repo. ESM-only, Node ≥ 24, British English throughout.
- **Works today (shipped + tested):** disclosure-on-event policy, on-device
  geofences, no-report zones, SOS/pick-me-up, night-out ephemeral sharing,
  dead-man's-switch check-ins, rendezvous + fair meeting point, spoken pick-up
  verification with silent duress word, gift-wrapped transport (NIP-59,
  unconditional), multi-relay fan-out, offline vector basemap, battery-aware
  cadence. All foreground, via the PWA (live at https://flock.forgesworn.dev/).
- **Does not work yet:** background breach detection with the app closed. No
  web/PWA platform can do background geofencing (2026); it requires the native
  Capacitor shell, which is scaffold-only (`native/`), gated on a GrapheneOS
  hardware spike (Phase 0).
- **Every signal is NIP-59 gift-wrapped** — this is unconditional, not a
  setting or a per-mode option.

## Privacy invariants (never break these — FLOCK.md §6)

1. Withholding location must be observationally identical to sharing.
2. A duress trigger must look identical to normal use; duress vocabulary is
   generative, never a fixed list.
3. Beacon and duress payloads use distinct derived keys — never shared.
4. Geofence membership is evaluated on-device; raw coordinates never leave the
   device except as an encrypted beacon after a triggering event.

## Library modules (`src/`) — pure, framework-free, no I/O

Each is importable as a subpath (`@forgesworn/flock/<module>`); the root import
re-exports all of them plus the full `canary-kit` + `canary-kit/nostr` surface.

- `geofence` — circle/polygon fences, `isBreach` (single fence), accuracy-aware
  containment. Multi-fence helpers live in `policy`.
- `policy` — the disclosure-on-event decision (`decideEmission`); precedence
  `help > pickup > breach > night-out coarse > withhold`.
- `signals` — `beacon`/`breach`/`pickup` + `help` duress → unsigned kind-20078
  events; builders derive keys and encrypt internally from the group seed.
- `nightout` — ephemeral groups (NIP-40 expiry), presence (derived: a member is
  "gone home" when their beacons go stale, default 600 s), separation outliers.
- `checkin` — dead-man's-switch: encrypted "I'm OK" heartbeats (any cadence),
  `ok`/`overdue`/`missed` classifier, local self-reminders (`selfCheckInStatus`),
  and "I've got this" acknowledgement + escalation (`buildAckSignal`,
  `classifyEscalation`) so a miss is handled by a person, not a single toast.
- `trail` — pre-SOS breadcrumbs: on-device rolling buffer (`pushCrumb`),
  disclosed only with a help/breach as a duress-keyed `trail` signal; no-report
  zones excluded at record time.
- `noreport` — inverse fences that cap disclosure over sensitive addresses.
- `buzz` — encrypted "look at your phone" ping. `allclear` — stand-down with a
  coerced flag. `fences` — circle-wide safe-place sync (latest-wins).
- `rendezvous` — "be back by / meet at" with ETA + at-risk assessment.
- `meeting` — fair meeting-point request/share. `disband` — circle dissolution.
- `offgrid` — deliberate "going dark" pre-announcement. `spokenverify` —
  face-to-face pick-up verification words + silent duress word.

## API quick reference (exact signatures)

```ts
// geofence
isBreach(point: LatLng, fence: Geofence): boolean            // ONE fence
type Geofence = { kind: 'circle', centre: LatLng, radiusMetres: number }
              | { kind: 'polygon', vertices: LatLng[] }       // LatLng = { lat, lon }

// policy (multi-fence helpers live here, not in geofence)
isWithinAnyFence(point: LatLng, fences: Geofence[]): boolean
decideEmission(ctx: EmissionContext, precisions?: Partial<EmissionPrecisions>): EmissionPlan
// EmissionContext: { mode: 'family'|'nightout', position?: LatLng|null,
//   geofences?: Geofence[], trigger?: 'none'|'pickup'|'help', accuracyMetres?: number,
//   offGrid?: boolean, noReportZones?: NoReportZone[] }
// EmissionPlan: { action: 'withhold'|'coarse'|'full', precision: number /* 0 when withholding */,
//   reason: 'none'|'nightout'|'breach'|'pickup'|'help' }

// signals — async; encryption + key derivation happen inside the builder
buildLocationSignal(p: { groupId: string, seedHex: string, signalType: 'beacon'|'breach'|'pickup',
  geohash: string, precision: number }): Promise<UnsignedEvent>
buildHelpSignal(p: { groupId: string, seedHex: string, member: string,
  location: { geohash: string, precision: number, locationSource: 'beacon'|'verifier' } | null,
  scope?: 'group'|'persona'|'master', originGroupId?: string }): Promise<UnsignedEvent>
signalTypeForReason(reason: EmissionReason): SignalType | null

// nightout — presence is DERIVED from beacon staleness; there is no "mark gone home" setter
nightOutExpiry(startedAt: number, durationSeconds: number): number
buildNightOutGroupEvent(p: { groupId: string, members: string[], encryptedContent: string,
  startedAt: number, durationSeconds: number, rotationInterval?: number, tolerance?: number }): UnsignedEvent
classifyPresence(beacons: MemberBeacon[], now: number, opts?: { staleAfterSeconds?: number }): PresenceEntry[]
stillOut(entries: PresenceEntry[]): PresenceEntry[]

// checkin
buildCheckInSignal(p: { groupId: string, seedHex: string, member: string,
  intervalSeconds: number, timestamp?: number,
  battery?: 'low'|'critical' }): Promise<UnsignedEvent>  // interval <= 0 stands down
classifyCheckins(checkins: CheckIn[], now: number, opts?: { graceSeconds?: number }): CheckInState[]
selfCheckInStatus(mine: CheckIn | null, now: number, opts?: { remindBeforeSeconds?: number,
  graceSeconds?: number }): 'none'|'ok'|'due-soon'|'overdue'|'missed'  // LOCAL only — never emits
buildAckSignal(p: { groupId: string, seedHex: string, member: string, target: string,
  timestamp?: number }): Promise<UnsignedEvent>  // "I've got this" for target's missed check-in
classifyEscalation(states: CheckInState[], acks: CheckInAck[], now: number,
  opts?: { stepSeconds?: number }): EscalationState[]  // unacknowledged first, levels 0-2

// trail — buffer is memory-only; disclosure only rides a help/breach
pushCrumb(crumbs: Breadcrumb[], crumb: Breadcrumb, opts?: { maxCrumbs?: number,
  maxAgeSeconds?: number, minSpacingSeconds?: number }): Breadcrumb[]  // pure; skips too-soon/older crumbs
buildTrailSignal(p: { groupId: string, seedHex: string, member: string,
  reason: 'help'|'breach', crumbs: Breadcrumb[], timestamp?: number }): Promise<UnsignedEvent>  // DURESS key
// Breadcrumb = { geohash: string, precision: number, timestamp: number }
```

Geohash encoding is the caller's job (`geohash-kit`'s `encode(lat, lon, precision)`);
signing is the caller's job (e.g. `nostr-tools` `finalizeEvent`). Group creation
(`createGroup`, `buildGroupStateEvent`) comes from `canary-kit`, re-exported at
the root. See `examples/quickstart.ts` for the full pipeline.

**Gift-wrapping is mandatory before publish** (FLOCK.md §3.3): the library has
no wrap helper — the PWA wraps every signal via `app/src/giftwrap.ts`
(`giftWrap(signer, recipientPk, inner, routeTag?)` → signed kind-1059, NIP-40
expiry baked in); an external consumer can use `nostr-tools`'s `nip59` module.
Publishing an unwrapped signal violates the privacy model.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): overview, module status, the make-or-break platform constraint
- [FLOCK.md](FLOCK.md): protocol spec — event kinds, payload shapes, privacy invariants
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): the stack and why each piece was chosen
- [docs/PRIVACY.md](docs/PRIVACY.md): relay threat model, gift-wrap-everything, no-report zones
- [docs/ROADMAP.md](docs/ROADMAP.md): the tracked backlog — the single source of truth for feature state
- [docs/FORGESWORN-TOOLKIT.md](docs/FORGESWORN-TOOLKIT.md): how flock maps onto the ForgeSworn toolset
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): agent instructions — commands, structure, security-critical paths

## Conventions

British English. ESM-only, ES2022. TDD — failing test first. Library modules
stay pure (return new state, no mutation). Geohash encoding + encryption stay
at the edge. Commits: `type: description`, no Co-Authored-By lines.
NIP-44 encryption (never NIP-04); NIP-40 `expiration` tag (never `expiry`).
